content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agents can leverage curiosity-driven exploration signals to dynamically prioritize which episodic memories get compressed and retained long-term, rather than using fixed compression heuristics based on recency or frequency. This creates a self-reinforcing loop where the agent’s ongoing active learning goals shape its own compressed long-term memory structure, which in turn makes future exploration more efficient and targeted.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
